title: First Marathon in 6 Months: Plan + Kit Under $800
summary: You don't need a lot — you need the right few things and a real plan. I built this around Hal Higdon's Novice 1 (the gold-standard plan for someone going from 5 miles to 26.2), with shoes as the biggest line item and everything else stripped to essentials. I assumed mid-tier quality and that you're road-running in a temperate climate — if you're in extreme heat/cold or trail running, tell me and I'll swap pieces. Total lands at ~$655, leaving you ~$150 of headroom for a race entry, replacement shoes around month 4, or extra apparel.

sections.name: The Plan (read this first)
sections.purpose: The training framework that makes the gear matter — free, proven, and built for your exact starting point.

products.name: Novice 1 Marathon Training Program (free online + companion app $5)
products.why_this: You're running 3x/week at 5 miles max — Novice 1 is literally designed for runners in exactly your position, ramping over 18 weeks with a peak long run of 20 miles. Six months gives you a comfortable buffer to start the 18-week plan with 2-3 weeks of base-building first.

sections.name: Shoes — the only gear that really matters
sections.purpose: Two pairs rotated to reduce injury risk during the high-mileage block; this is the single most important purchase.

products.name: Ghost 16
products.why_this: The default 'safe' daily trainer for first-time marathoners — neutral, cushioned, and the most-recommended shoe by run coaches for someone going from 5 to 20+ mile long runs. Buy a half-size up from your normal size for swelling on long runs.

products.name: Clifton 9
products.why_this: Your second rotation pair — max cushion for long-run days and recovery runs. Rotating two different shoes is proven to reduce overuse injuries, which is the #1 risk for first-time marathoners stepping up mileage.

sections.name: Tracking & pacing
sections.purpose: A real GPS watch so you can run by pace and heart rate, not guesswork — critical when long runs get to 2-3+ hours.

products.name: Forerunner 165
products.why_this: The sweet-spot marathon training watch in 2026: full GPS, heart rate, race predictor, daily suggested workouts, and 19hr GPS battery (covers your longest training run easily). Skip the Forerunner 265 — you don't need the extras yet.

sections.name: Run apparel essentials
sections.purpose: Just enough to run year-round without chafing, blisters, or buying things you don't need.

products.name: Hidden Comfort No-Show Socks (3-pack)
products.why_this: Blisters end marathon training. Balega Hidden Comfort is the most-recommended running sock by run shops — get three pairs so you always have a clean dry pair for long-run day.

products.name: Original Anti-Chafe Balm
products.why_this: Non-negotiable for any run over ~10 miles. Apply to inner thighs, underarms, and (for guys) nipples before every long run. You will thank me at mile 16.

products.name: QuickStart 2.0 6L Hydration Vest
products.why_this: Once your long runs cross 90 minutes you need to carry water and fuel. The Nathan QuickStart is the best entry-level vest — light, simple, two soft flasks up front, room for gels and a phone.

sections.name: Fueling — practice this like a workout
sections.purpose: Your gut needs training as much as your legs. Start using these on long runs from week 3.

products.name: Gel 100 (12-pack)
products.why_this: The gel pros actually use — hydrogel tech that sits easy on the stomach, which matters when you're new to fueling on the run. Take one every 30-40 min on runs over 75 min.

products.name: Electrolyte Drink Mix (30-stick variety pack)
products.why_this: Heavy sweat + long runs = cramps and bonking if you don't replace sodium. One stick in your vest flask on any run over an hour, especially as summer training hits.

sections.name: Recovery (so you can run again tomorrow)
sections.purpose: Cheap, high-leverage tools that prevent the small injuries that derail first-time marathoners.

products.name: GRID Foam Roller
products.why_this: 10 minutes a night on quads, calves, and IT band is the single best free injury prevention. The GRID is firmer and lasts longer than the cheap smooth rollers.

products.name: Resistance Band Loop Set (5-pack)
products.why_this: Glute and hip activation work prevents the knee and IT band issues that take down ~30% of first-time marathoners. 10 min of band work before runs, 2-3x a week.

products.name: HRM-Dual Chest Strap (optional add-on for accurate HR zones)
products.why_this: Wrist HR is unreliable for zone training. If you want to actually run easy days easy (the #1 mistake new marathoners make), pair this with the Forerunner 165. Skip if you're feeling tight on budget — the watch alone is fine to start.

next_steps: Get a proper gait analysis at a local running store (Fleet Feet, Road Runner Sports) before buying shoes — they'll confirm whether the Ghost is right for your foot strike.
Start Hal Higdon's Novice 1 plan this week; rotate your two shoe pairs from day one so each gets recovery time between runs.
Book your goal marathon now (Nov 2026 race) so the 18-week plan lands you at race day — and sign up for one tune-up half-marathon around week 12.
